doc: TB-303-style monophonic acid bass voice: a saw oscillator through a resonant lowpass whose cutoff is swept by the amp envelope, so every plucked note squelches open then snaps shut -- the classic acid bassline timbre. One envelope drives both the filter sweep and the amplitude, giving the short, accented character of a 303. Hosted by a 1-voice voicer; the sequencer's degree notes resolve through the host's harmony into `freq`. interface: freq/gate in, audio/active out.

nodes.doc: Saw core -- rich harmonics for the resonant filter to carve.
Snappy 303 contour (linear CV): fast attack, short decay, low sustain. Drives BOTH the filter sweep and the VCA.
Env CV [0,1] -> filter cutoff [160,2800] Hz (exponential). This is the acid squelch: the filter opens on the attack and closes as the note decays.
Resonant lowpass -- high resonance for the whistling acid tone; cutoff rides the env sweep.
Shape the linear env CV into an x-squared perceptual volume curve for the VCA.
VCA: filtered saw (a) * shaped amp CV (b).
